Home.seoOverview: JoinMyPDF is a privacy-focused suite of browser-based tools for PDF, image, and developer workflows. Every task runs through local processing in your browser—files stay on your device with no mandatory uploads to our servers. Our enterprise-grade architecture supports offline-capable workflows where supported, so you can merge, compress, convert, and secure documents with maximum control. Built for professionals who need fast results without sacrificing security or compliance.

upload.q: Is my data uploaded to servers?
upload.a: No. Files and data are processed locally on your device in the browser. They are not uploaded to our servers for standard tool workflows.

free.a: Yes. All tools are free to use and run entirely in your browser—no account is required for typical workflows.

professional.q: Are these tools safe for professional use?
professional.a: Yes. By processing files locally and offering metadata removal workflows, we help teams handle sensitive documents with maximum security and control.

image.title: About Our Image Tools
image.p1: Every image tool on this page runs entirely in your browser. When you resize, crop, compress, or convert a photo, the file is processed locally on your device using WebAssembly and the Canvas API — nothing is uploaded to a server. That means your screenshots, ID scans, and personal photos never leave your computer, and there is no upload queue to wait for.
image.p2: Because processing happens on your own hardware, results are instant even for large images, the tools work offline once the page has loaded, and there are no file-size limits imposed by server plans. Use them for quick one-off edits or batch-prepare images for the web, social posts, or print without installing any software.

faq.q1: Are my images uploaded to a server?
faq.a1: No. All image processing happens locally in your browser. You can verify this in your browser's DevTools network tab — no image data is transmitted while a tool runs.
faq.q2: Is there a file-size or usage limit?
faq.a2: There are no artificial limits. The only constraint is your device's memory, so even very large photos and batches process fine on a typical laptop or phone.
faq.q3: Do the image tools reduce quality?
faq.a3: Only when you ask them to. Compression tools let you control the quality level, while converters and crop or resize tools preserve as much quality as the target format allows.
faq.a4: Yes. Once a tool page has loaded, processing runs on your device, so an interrupted connection won't stop an edit that is already in progress.

security.title: About Our Security Tools
security.p1: Protect your information with our dedicated security suite. Whether you are adding passwords to PDFs, redacting sensitive content, or removing metadata, our tools provide essential privacy features directly in your browser. Since we process everything locally, your secure files never leave your possession.

faq.q1: Is redaction truly permanent?
faq.a1: Yes, our redaction tools permanently mask the selected areas to ensure sensitive content cannot be recovered.
faq.q2: How do I know my passwords are safe?
faq.a2: Passwords used for encryption are processed locally and are never stored or transmitted by our tools.
faq.q3: Can I remove hidden metadata from my files?
faq.a3: Yes, our metadata wiper tool is designed to strip EXIF and other hidden data from your files before you share them.

audio.title: About Our Audio Tools
audio.p1: Convert, trim, compress, and edit audio directly in your browser. These tools use ffmpeg compiled to WebAssembly, so the same engine trusted by professional media pipelines runs locally on your device — your recordings, voice notes, and music files are never uploaded.
audio.p2: Local processing means no upload wait for large files, no privacy trade-off for sensitive recordings, and no subscription. Trimming and format conversion use stream copy where possible, which is both instant and lossless.

faq.q1: Are my audio files uploaded anywhere?
faq.a1: No. The ffmpeg engine runs inside your browser via WebAssembly, so files are read and written entirely on your device.
faq.q2: Will trimming or converting reduce audio quality?
faq.a2: Trimming uses stream copy whenever possible, which is lossless. Format conversions let you choose the bitrate so you control the quality/size trade-off.
faq.q3: How large a file can I process?
faq.a3: There is no fixed limit — capacity depends on your device's memory. Typical podcasts, songs, and voice memos process without issues.

developer.title: About Our Developer Tools
developer.p1: Streamline your development workflow with our suite of professional developer tools. From encoding and hashing to system utility tasks, all operations are performed directly in your browser. By utilizing local processing, we ensure your source code, configuration files, and sensitive credentials never leave your secure development environment.

faq.q1: Are these tools suitable for production-level tasks?
faq.a1: Yes, our tools are built for precision and reliability, making them ideal for both rapid prototyping and production debugging.
faq.q2: Do you store any of my input data?
faq.a2: No, because we use a zero-upload architecture, your code and data remain strictly local to your browser.
faq.q3: Can I use these tools in an offline development environment?
faq.a3: Once the page is loaded, the tools operate entirely without external dependencies.

data-conversion.title: About Our Data Conversion Tools
data-conversion.p1: Transform your data formats with confidence. Our specialized conversion tools handle structured data types, ensuring accurate mapping and fast execution. By keeping all conversion tasks within your browser, we guarantee that your proprietary data and structured schemas remain private and secure throughout the entire process.

faq.q1: What types of structured data are supported?
faq.a1: We support common data formats used for programmatic and analytical workflows.
faq.q2: Is the data conversion accurate?
faq.a2: Yes, we utilize robust parsing logic to ensure that your data structure remains valid during and after conversion.
faq.q3: Do I need to be a developer to use these?
faq.a3: No, our tools feature intuitive interfaces designed for users of all technical levels.

productivity.title: About Our Productivity Tools
productivity.p1: Boost your daily efficiency with our collection of essential productivity utilities. From text formatting and data sorting to quick calculations, our tools help you save time on repetitive tasks. Everything runs directly on your machine, ensuring your workflow data remains private and your focus stays entirely on your projects.

faq.q1: Are these tools suitable for business use?
faq.a1: Yes, our tools are designed to be fast, reliable, and privacy-focused, making them great for professional environments.
faq.q2: Can I use these on any device?
faq.a2: Our tools are web-based and responsive, working seamlessly on any device with a modern browser.
faq.q3: Is there a limit to how many tasks I can perform?
faq.a3: There are no artificial limits; you can use our productivity tools as often as your workflow requires.

network.title: About Our Network & API Tools
network.p1: Analyze, test, and debug your network and API endpoints with our browser-based suite. Whether you are inspecting HTTP requests, validating API payloads, or encoding network data, all tools execute locally on your device. Keep your sensitive API keys and internal network configurations secure by avoiding external server uploads.

faq.q1: Do these tools support secure HTTPS endpoints?
faq.a1: Yes, our tools are compatible with standard network protocols used in modern web development.
faq.q2: Is my API data stored by the tool?
faq.a2: No, we strictly follow a zero-upload policy; your data is processed only within your browser memory.
faq.q3: Can I use this for testing local network services?
faq.a3: Yes, our tools can interact with any endpoint accessible from your browser, including local development servers.

unit-converters.title: About Our Unit Converters Tools
unit-converters.p1: Need fast and accurate unit conversions? Our converter suite covers everything from weight and length to digital storage and data transfer speeds. By running locally in your browser, these tools provide instant results without requiring an internet connection after the initial load, making them a reliable utility for any workflow.

faq.q1: How accurate are the conversions?
faq.a1: We use standard scientific conversion factors to ensure high precision in every calculation.
faq.q2: Can I convert custom or obscure units?
faq.a2: Our suite focuses on the most commonly used global standards for maximum utility.
faq.q3: Do these converters work offline?
faq.a3: Yes, once the tool is loaded, you can perform conversions completely offline.

favicon.title: About Our Favicon Tools
favicon.p1: Generate the perfect favicon for your website in seconds. Our tools automatically create all necessary sizes and formats for desktop and mobile browsers from your source images. Process your branding assets locally and securely, ensuring your identity designs remain private until you're ready to publish them.

faq.q1: Which formats do you generate?
faq.a1: We generate all industry-standard favicon formats for compatibility across all devices.
faq.q2: Can I use any image type as a source?
faq.a2: Yes, you can upload common formats like PNG, JPG, or SVG to create your favicon.
faq.q3: Is the process automated?
faq.a3: Yes, simply upload your source image, and we handle the resizing and formatting automatically.

text-json.title: About Our Text & JSON Tools
text-json.p1: Format, minify, diff, encode, and convert text and JSON without your data ever leaving the browser. API responses and config payloads often contain identifiers and secrets, so these tools process everything locally on your device.
text-json.p2: The editors handle large documents responsively, update results live as you type, and work offline once the page is loaded. No signup, no server, no limits.

faq.q1: Is it safe to paste API responses with real data?
faq.a1: Yes. Formatting, conversion, and diffing run locally in your browser — nothing is transmitted or logged.
faq.q2: How large a JSON document can I format?
faq.a2: Documents of several megabytes format quickly since there is no upload. The practical limit is your browser's memory.
faq.q3: What's the difference between encoding and encrypting?
faq.a3: Base64 and URL encoding are reversible representations, not security measures. Anyone can decode them — use proper encryption for anything confidential.

utilities.title: About Our Utilities
utilities.p1: This hub collects browser-based utilities — favicon generators, JSON and text processors, encoders, and more — that all share one principle: your files and text are processed locally on your device, never uploaded.
utilities.p2: That local-first design makes the tools fast, private, and dependable: no upload queues, no account walls, and full functionality even on a flaky connection once the page has loaded.

faq.q1: Why are browser-based tools more private?
faq.a1: Because processing happens on your device, there is no server that could store, scan, or leak your data. You can confirm this with your browser's network inspector.
faq.q2: Are these tools really free?
faq.a2: Yes. Since there are no server processing costs, the tools are free without file limits, watermarks, or trial periods.

pdf.title: About Our PDF Tools
pdf.p1: Our browser-based PDF tools offer a privacy-first way to manage your documents. From merging and splitting to optimizing and editing, all tasks are performed locally on your device. Since no files are uploaded to a server, your sensitive documents—like contracts, receipts, and personal forms—remain completely secure and under your control at all times.

faq.q1: Is it safe to edit PDF documents online?
faq.a1: Yes, with JoinMyPDF, your files never leave your device, ensuring total privacy.
faq.q2: Are there limits on the number of pages I can process?
faq.a2: No, because processing happens on your local hardware, there are no server-side limits.
faq.q3: Do these tools work with scanned PDFs?
faq.a3: Yes, our tools can handle both text-based and scanned PDF documents seamlessly.

video.title: About Our Video Tools
video.p1: Manage your video files instantly with our comprehensive suite of video editing tools. Whether you need to trim, convert, or compress, everything happens in your browser using the power of your own hardware. Forget about long upload queues and privacy concerns; your raw video footage stays strictly on your computer throughout the entire editing process.

faq.q1: Will editing videos consume a lot of data?
faq.a1: No, since all processing is local, you don't need to upload or download large video files.
faq.q2: Does this tool support high-resolution video?
faq.a2: Yes, our browser-based engine is optimized to handle large, high-definition video files efficiently.
faq.q3: Is the video editing process fast?
faq.a3: Since there is no server-side bottleneck, you get immediate results as soon as the tool loads.

mp4.title: About Our MP4 Tools
mp4.p1: Specialized tools for all your MP4 needs. JoinMyPDF provides dedicated utilities to edit, convert, and manage your MP4 files directly in your browser. By utilizing your device's local processing power, we ensure that your media files remain private and that you don't waste time uploading massive files to a cloud server.

faq.q1: Can I convert MP4 to other formats?
faq.a1: Yes, our dedicated MP4 suite includes various conversion options to meet your needs.
faq.q2: Does this tool require high-end hardware?
faq.a2: No, the tools are optimized to run smoothly on standard laptops and desktops.
faq.q3: Are there file-size limitations for MP4 files?
faq.a3: Unlike server-based tools, we do not impose file-size limits on your local hardware.

convert.title: About Our Convert Tools
convert.p1: Experience the fastest way to convert files between formats with our Zero-Upload conversion suite. From documents to media, all conversions are executed locally in your browser. This approach guarantees maximum security for your personal data and eliminates the waiting time typically associated with cloud-based file conversion services.

faq.q1: What file formats are supported?
faq.a1: We support a wide range of common formats for documents, images, and media.
faq.q2: Is my data kept private during conversion?
faq.a2: Absolutely; since no files are uploaded, your data is never accessible to anyone else.
faq.q3: Is this conversion service truly free?
faq.a3: Yes, all our browser-based conversion tools are free to use without server plans.

compress.title: About Our Compress Tools
compress.p1: Shrink your files without sacrificing quality using our specialized local compression utilities. Our tools leverage WebAssembly to compress documents and media right inside your browser. By avoiding server uploads, you not only keep your data private but also avoid the bandwidth restrictions and file-size caps common in traditional online compression tools.

faq.q1: Does compression reduce the visual quality of images?
faq.a1: We use advanced algorithms to maximize compression while maintaining high visual fidelity.
faq.q2: Can I compress multiple files at once?
faq.a2: Yes, our tools support batch-processing to save you time on multiple files.
faq.q3: Do I need an internet connection to compress files?
faq.a3: Once the page is loaded, the tools work entirely offline, providing maximum flexibility.

extract.title: About Our Extract Tools
extract.p1: Need to pull specific elements from your files? Our extraction suite is designed to help you quickly pull pages, images, or data from documents and media. Everything is processed locally in your browser, ensuring that even when you are extracting sensitive information, your files never leave your device.

faq.q1: Can I extract specific pages from a PDF?
faq.a1: Yes, our tool allows you to select and extract any page range you need.
faq.q2: Is the extraction process lossless?
faq.a2: Yes, we ensure that extracted images and data retain their original quality.
faq.q3: Does this work on password-protected files?
faq.a3: You can process protected files as long as you have the necessary permissions to open them.

jpg.title: About Our JPG Tools
jpg.p1: Optimize and transform your JPG files effortlessly with our specialized browser-based tools. From compression and format conversion to metadata cleaning, all operations run on your own hardware. Keep your photo collection private and enjoy instant results without any server upload overhead.

faq.q1: Can I convert other image formats to JPG?
faq.a1: Yes, our suite includes comprehensive conversion options for various image types.
faq.q2: Will the quality decrease when I save the JPG?
faq.a2: You maintain full control over the quality settings to ensure the best balance for your needs.
faq.q3: Are these tools suitable for batch processing?
faq.a3: Absolutely, our JPG suite is optimized for handling multiple images simultaneously.

png.title: About Our PNG Tools
png.p1: Create, edit, and optimize high-quality PNGs with our dedicated toolset. Whether you need transparent backgrounds, file compression, or format conversion, our browser-based approach provides speed and privacy. Since all tasks are handled locally, your graphics and design assets stay secure on your machine.

faq.q1: Can I compress PNG files without losing transparency?
faq.a1: Yes, our compression algorithms are designed to keep transparency intact.
faq.q2: Are these tools compatible with all browsers?
faq.a2: Yes, our tools are built with web-standard technologies that work on all modern browsers.
faq.q3: Is there a limit to how many PNGs I can edit?
faq.a3: No, you are only limited by your device's memory.

mp3.title: About Our MP3 Tools
mp3.p1: Manage your audio files directly in the browser with our efficient MP3 toolset. Trim tracks, change formats, and merge audio clips without ever uploading a file to a server. Enjoy the peace of mind that comes with knowing your personal music and recordings stay entirely on your local machine.

faq.q1: Can I trim an MP3 file without re-encoding?
faq.a1: Yes, our tool supports fast, quality-preserving cuts for audio tracks.
faq.q2: Can I merge different audio formats into an MP3?
faq.a2: Yes, our conversion and merging features handle various audio inputs seamlessly.
faq.q3: Does it work offline?
faq.a3: Yes, after the initial page load, all audio processing works fully offline.

yaml.title: About Our YAML Tools
yaml.p1: Master your YAML configurations with our dedicated editing and validation tools. Whether you are structuring complex config files for cloud infrastructure or managing local application settings, our browser-based tools offer instant syntax checking and formatting. Experience secure, private editing without the risk of exposing your configurations to external servers.

faq.q1: Does the tool support multi-document YAML files?
faq.a1: Yes, our parser is designed to handle standard YAML structures efficiently.
faq.q2: Is my YAML data safe?
faq.a2: Your YAML data is processed locally in your browser memory and never transmitted to our servers.
faq.q3: Can I format minified YAML?
faq.a3: Yes, our formatter is capable of transforming minified or poorly structured YAML into clean, readable code.

json.title: About Our JSON Tools
json.p1: JSON management made simple and secure. Our suite includes powerful tools for formatting, minifying, and validating JSON structures. By processing your data locally, we provide a safe environment for handling API responses, configuration data, and large datasets without ever needing to upload them to the cloud.

faq.q1: Can I validate large JSON files?
faq.a1: Yes, our client-side engine is optimized to handle large JSON payloads quickly and accurately.
faq.q2: Is there a limit on the number of JSON keys I can process?
faq.a2: No, the tool handles complex and deeply nested JSON structures without arbitrary limits.
faq.q3: Can I convert JSON to other formats?
faq.a3: Our JSON suite includes conversion options to common formats for improved interoperability.

text.title: About Our Text Tools
text.p1: Enhance your text editing and manipulation tasks with our versatile collection of text utilities. From character counts and case conversion to advanced cleaning and formatting, all tasks run locally on your device. Keep your drafts, notes, and sensitive text content completely private with our zero-upload promise.

faq.q1: Can I process very long documents?
faq.a1: Yes, our text tools are optimized for both short snippets and large text documents.
faq.q2: Do you support different text encodings?
faq.a2: Yes, we prioritize standard UTF-8 encoding for maximum compatibility.
faq.q3: Is this tool suitable for sensitive writing projects?
faq.a3: Absolutely; since no data is transmitted, your content remains on your computer at all times.

word.title: About Our Word Tools
word.p1: Manage your Microsoft Word documents effectively with our specialized browser-based utilities. Whether you need to convert files, extract text, or perform quick edits, our tools process your documents directly on your machine. This guarantees your document privacy while delivering the fast, reliable results you expect from professional-grade software.

faq.q1: Does the tool support all .docx features?
faq.a1: Our tools focus on core document management and conversion tasks for high compatibility.
faq.q2: Will my document formatting be preserved?
faq.a2: We strive to maintain original document structure during processing to ensure your files look exactly as intended.
faq.q3: Can I use this for confidential business documents?
faq.a3: Yes, the zero-upload nature of our tools ensures your confidential documents never leave your local device.

rotate.title: About Our Rotate Tools
rotate.p1: Need to fix the orientation of your documents or images? Our rotation suite provides precise, instant tools to flip and rotate files exactly as you need them. Because all processing occurs locally within your browser, your sensitive files remain private, and you benefit from high-speed results without any upload delays.

faq.q1: Can I rotate multiple images at once?
faq.a1: Yes, our batch rotation features allow you to adjust multiple files simultaneously.
faq.q2: Is the rotation permanent?
faq.a2: Yes, once you apply the rotation and download the file, the orientation is saved permanently.
faq.q3: Will the file quality be affected?
faq.a3: No, our tools perform lossless rotations to ensure your file clarity is perfectly preserved.

crop.title: About Our Crop Tools
crop.p1: Focus on what matters by cropping your files with ease. Whether it’s trimming margins from a document or selecting a specific region of an image, our browser-based tools give you full control. Processing happens locally on your own hardware, so your personal files are never uploaded or stored anywhere else.

faq.q1: Can I crop images to custom dimensions?
faq.a1: Yes, you can freely adjust the crop area or specify exact pixel dimensions.
faq.q2: Do these tools support PDF cropping?
faq.a2: Absolutely; you can easily trim white margins or unwanted areas from your PDF pages.
faq.q3: Is the cropping process reversible?
faq.a3: You can adjust your crop selection as many times as you like before confirming the final download.

excel.title: About Our Excel Tools
excel.p1: Manage your spreadsheet data securely with our browser-based Excel utilities. From conversion to data extraction, all tasks are handled on your local device. This architecture ensures that your private business data, financial records, and structured lists stay entirely under your control, free from the risks of cloud-based processing.

faq.q1: Are formulas preserved during conversion?
faq.a1: Our tools focus on structured data management; conversion outputs are optimized for data integrity.
faq.q2: Can I process large Excel files?
faq.a2: Yes, our local processing engine is designed to handle large datasets efficiently within your browser's memory.
faq.q3: Is my data ever uploaded to a server?
faq.a3: No, we maintain a strict zero-upload policy for all spreadsheet operations.

article.title: Why stripping image metadata protects your privacy
article.intro: Modern cameras and phones embed far more than pixels. EXIF and related metadata can reveal where a photo was taken, which device captured it, when it was shot, and which software touched it. A privacy-friendly image sanitizer lets you strip that trail before you publish, send, or archive photos — without uploading files to a remote cleaner.

exifRisks.heading: EXIF risks: location tracking and digital fingerprints
exifRisks.p1: GPS coordinates in photo EXIF can expose home addresses, travel patterns, or client sites. Even without maps open, many social platforms and messaging apps retain original files long enough for someone with export access to recover coordinates.
exifRisks.p2: Device model, serial-adjacent lens data, and software tags create a fingerprint that can link photos across posts. Stripping metadata does not anonymize faces, but it removes easy machine-readable identifiers that travel with the file.
exifRisks.p3: Capture timestamps can also leak schedules. If you share screenshots or product shots captured on-site, wiping date and GPS together is a practical digital-safety habit.
exifRisks.p4: Removing EXIF is essential whenever an image leaves a trusted device — portfolio sites, marketplaces, tickets, email, and public CDNs.

howItWorks.heading: How this privacy wiper works
howItWorks.p1: After you drop a JPG or PNG, JoinMyPDF reads metadata locally with an EXIF parser and lists findings such as GPS location or device model.
howItWorks.p2: Wipe & Download Clean Image removes EXIF for JPEG without re-encoding pixels, and strips PNG text/time/EXIF chunks while keeping image data intact — so dimensions and visual quality stay the same.
howItWorks.p3: Nothing is uploaded. Large photos are processed in your browser; keep one file at a time for the smoothest experience on constrained devices.
howItWorks.p4: Need to edit copyright fields instead of wiping everything? Use the Image Metadata Editor tool for surgical EXIF changes.

howToUse.heading: How to use the Image Metadata Wiper
howToUse.p1: Drop a JPG or PNG into the zone, or select a file from your device.
howToUse.p2: Review the detected metadata list. If GPS or device tags appear, treat the file as sensitive until wiped.
howToUse.p3: Click Wipe & Download Clean Image. Your browser downloads a cleaned copy; the rating panel unlocks after a successful wipe and download.
howToUse.p4: Clear resets the session on shared computers. Prefer clean downloads over re-sharing the original attachment.

privacy.heading: Why local-first metadata wiping matters
privacy.p1: Photos often contain personal or client context that should never sit in a third-party upload log. A local stripper keeps bytes in the page process.
privacy.p2: Local-first also means no account wall and no per-file upload quotas for everyday sanitizing.
privacy.p3: Combine wiping with compress or resize only after privacy cleanup if you need both smaller files and clean metadata.
privacy.p4: Used regularly, a private image sanitizer becomes part of a safer publish-and-share workflow alongside JoinMyPDF’s other local tools.

article.title: Screen DPI vs print DPI — and why print shops care
article.intro: DPI (dots per inch) tells layout and print software how to map pixels onto physical paper. Updating density metadata is not the same as resizing an image. This online DPI changer writes the tag print workflows expect — especially 300 DPI — while leaving the photo’s pixel grid untouched.

howToUse.heading: How to use this DPI converter for print
howToUse.p1: Drop a JPG or PNG. The tool reads any existing density tag and shows current DPI alongside pixel dimensions.
howToUse.p2: Choose a preset (72, 150, 300, 600), enter a custom DPI, or click Print Ready to lock in the 300 DPI print standard.
howToUse.p3: Click Convert & Download. Your browser saves a copy with updated EXIF/JFIF (JPEG) or pHYs (PNG) — no upload, no re-encode of pixels.
howToUse.p4: Rating and review stay hidden until a successful Convert & Download.

screenVsPrint.heading: 72 DPI screens vs 300 DPI print
screenVsPrint.p1: Many web exports default near 72 or 96 DPI because screens are addressed in pixels, not ink dots. That tag is fine for on-screen work.
screenVsPrint.p2: Commercial print pipelines usually expect ~300 DPI metadata so InDesign, RIP software, and prepress can estimate physical size correctly.
screenVsPrint.p3: If a photo looks soft when printed large, you may need more pixels (use Resize Image) — changing DPI alone does not invent detail.
screenVsPrint.p4: Use Print Ready when a vendor asks for “300 DPI files” and your artwork already has enough resolution for the intended print size.

metadata.heading: What this tool changes (and what it does not)
metadata.p1: JPEG: density is written into EXIF resolution tags and JFIF APP0 when present.
metadata.p2: PNG: density is stored in the pHYs chunk as pixels-per-meter (converted from DPI).
metadata.p3: Pixel width, height, and compressed image data stay the same — you will not get a pixelated-looking file from this step alone.
metadata.p4: For privacy stripping or copyright EXIF fields, use the Metadata Wiper or Metadata Editor instead.

privacy.heading: Why local-first DPI conversion matters
privacy.p1: Print proofs and client photos should not hop through a remote “DPI fixer” API just to change a header tag.
privacy.p2: Processing stays in your browser with piexif-style EXIF updates and direct PNG chunk rewriting.
privacy.p3: No account wall for standard conversions.
privacy.p4: Pair with Resize Image only when the print size demands more or fewer pixels — then re-tag DPI if needed.

article.title: Split images for Instagram grids and aesthetic collages
article.intro: An online image splitter turns one strong photo into a set of matching tiles. Creators use this for Instagram grid drops, carousel storytelling, and mood-board collages — without installing desktop editors.

howToUse.heading: How to use this Instagram grid maker
howToUse.p1: Drop a JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, or HEIC. Prefer a high-resolution source if tiles will be viewed large.
howToUse.p2: Pick a preset (2×2, 3×3, 1×3, 3×1) or enter custom rows and columns (1–10).
howToUse.p3: Check the live cut preview — dashed lines and tile numbers show posting order (left to right, top to bottom).
howToUse.p4: Click Slice & Download ZIP. Rating unlocks after a successful export.

instagram.heading: Instagram grid layouts and collages
instagram.p1: A classic profile aesthetic uses 3×3 so nine posts reconstruct one hero image on the profile grid.
instagram.p2: 1×3 strips work for panoramic carousels; 3×1 stacks work for tall stories or vertical narrative.
instagram.p3: Upload tiles in numbered order so the full composition reads correctly on the profile.
instagram.p4: For precise single crops instead of a full grid, use Crop Image.

quality.heading: Quality when you crop image into pieces
quality.p1: Tiles are drawn from the original bitmap with Canvas — no downscale unless you choose a denser grid on a tiny file.
quality.p2: PNG sources export lossless PNG tiles. JPEG sources export high-quality JPEG tiles to keep file sizes reasonable.
quality.p3: Remainder pixels land in the last row and column so nothing is discarded from the edges.
quality.p4: Very large grids on huge photos may take a moment; progress updates while tiles package into the ZIP.

privacy.heading: Why local-first splitting matters
privacy.p1: Campaign artwork and unreleased shoots should not upload to a remote “splitter” just to tile a canvas.
privacy.p2: Slicing and JSZip packaging stay on-device.
privacy.p3: No account wall for standard exports.
privacy.p4: Pair with Compress Image if the ZIP of tiles needs lighter uploads to social platforms.

article.title: Protect private data in screenshots before you share
article.intro: Support tickets, dashboards, and phone photos often include names, addresses, account numbers, and faces you never meant to publish. An image redactor that runs locally lets you blur screenshots online and censor images free — without uploading confidential pixels to a remote service.

howToUse.heading: How to use Image Blur & Redact
howToUse.p1: Drop a JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, or HEIC file into the upload zone. The canvas shows your image with a crosshair cursor.
howToUse.p2: Drag a rectangle over the area to hide. The floating toolbar offers Blur (with intensity), Pixelate, or Solid Black, with a live canvas preview.
howToUse.p3: Click Apply region for each marquee, then Process & Download Redacted Image. Rating and review unlock only after a successful download.
howToUse.p4: Use Undo / Clear all if you need another pass. Optional Auto-Detect Faces (Experimental) suggests blur regions in supported browsers.

protectScreenshots.heading: Common screenshot privacy hazards
protectScreenshots.p1: Customer names in CRM screenshots, email addresses in admin panels, and shipping addresses in order UIs are the usual leaks. Redact before pasting into chat or public docs.
protectScreenshots.p2: Faces in team photos and ID selfies deserve extra care. Prefer pixelate or strong blur when you must show context but hide identity.
protectScreenshots.p3: Account numbers and QR payment codes should usually get Solid Black — blur alone can sometimes leave recoverable shapes.
protectScreenshots.p4: After redacting visuals, consider wiping EXIF with the Metadata Wiper so location tags do not travel with the file.

modes.heading: Blur, pixelate, or solid black?
modes.p1: Blur softens detail while keeping layout context — good for UI demos where you still need to show structure.
modes.p2: Pixelate is the classic mosaic look for faces and logos when you want an obvious censor mark.
modes.p3: Solid Black is the strongest visual redaction for irreversible-looking boxes over text and numbers.
modes.p4: All modes rewrite pixels on a canvas locally. Downloads preserve JPEG/WebP quality when the source format allows.

privacy.heading: Why local-first redaction matters
privacy.p1: Cloud “blur my screenshot” tools create retention risk for the very secrets you are trying to hide. Keeping processing in the browser removes that upload step.
privacy.p2: JoinMyPDF never needs an account for this tool. Large images still depend on device memory — redact the crop you need when files are huge.
privacy.p3: Experimental face detection uses the browser Face Detector API when present; otherwise redact manually. Detection still happens on-device.
privacy.p4: Pair this tool with Redact PDF when the sensitive content lives in a document instead of a bitmap.

article.title: Why watermarking protects your copyright online
article.intro: Once a photo leaves your drive, it can be screenshotted, reuploaded, and sold without credit. A free image watermark tool will not replace a legal strategy, but it makes ownership visible and discourages casual reuse — especially when the mark is baked into pixels before delivery.

howToUse.heading: How to use this online image watermarker
howToUse.p1: Drop one or more JPG/PNG/WebP/HEIC files. Pick Text or Logo/Image in the sidebar.
howToUse.p2: For text, set message, font, color, size, and opacity. For logos, upload a transparent PNG and optionally click Auto-Scale Logo (about 10% of photo width).
howToUse.p3: Choose a grid position and fine-tune X/Y offsets. Watch the live preview, then Apply & Download.
howToUse.p4: Rating and review stay hidden until a successful export. Multiple files download as a ZIP with the same watermark settings.

copyright.heading: Copyright basics for photographers and brands
copyright.p1: Visible watermarks signal ownership on portfolio sites, social previews, and client proofs. Subtle opacity keeps the photo readable while still asserting credit.
copyright.p2: Logo marks work well for studios; text marks work for © Name Year stamps. Keep the mark consistent across a batch so published sets look intentional.
copyright.p3: Watermarking does not stop determined theft, but it raises the effort to remove branding and helps DMCA/context matching later.
copyright.p4: For PDF proofs, use the separate PDF watermark tool; keep this suite for photographic bitmaps.

logoText.heading: Text vs logo watermarks
logoText.p1: Text is fastest for copyright lines and Proofs — no extra asset required.
logoText.p2: Logos preserve brand color and shape. PNGs with alpha stay transparent on the canvas export.
logoText.p3: Auto-Scale Logo sizes the mark relative to each photo so a tall portrait and a wide landscape stay proportionally branded without hand-tweaking every file.
logoText.p4: Offsets let you nudge marks away from faces or product focal points after the grid position is set.

privacy.heading: Why local-first watermarking matters
privacy.p1: Client galleries and unpublished shoots should not hitchhike through a third-party watermark API. Canvas processing keeps files on-device.
privacy.p2: No account wall for standard exports. Very large batches still depend on browser memory — process in groups if needed.
privacy.p3: Originals on disk are untouched; downloads are new baked files.
privacy.p4: Pair with Image Metadata Wiper if you also need GPS/EXIF cleaned before publish.

howToUse.heading: How to use Rotate & Align

howToUse.p2: Use Quick Rotate for 90° left/right or 180°, or type an exact angle under Precision Rotation (for example 45°).

deskewScans.heading: Straighten scanned documents with Auto-Align
deskewScans.p1: Phone photos of paper often sit a few degrees off. Auto-Align scores candidate angles with a lightweight projection-profile pass — no upload and no heavy OCR dependency for the default path.
deskewScans.p2: A spinner and progress bar show while the browser estimates alignment. The detected micro-angle is applied on top of your current 90° orientation.
deskewScans.p3: Fine-tune with the degrees field if the automatic guess is close but not perfect. Re-run Auto-Align after replacing the source scan.
deskewScans.p4: For multi-page PDF orientation metadata, use Rotate PDF; use this suite when the scan is an image file.

precisionFlip.heading: Precision rotation and flip image online
precisionFlip.p1: Custom degrees matter for design layouts, mockups, and print bleeds that are not orthogonal. Preview updates as you apply the angle.
precisionFlip.p2: Flip controls mirror the canvas horizontally or vertically so you can correct selfie mirrors or upside captures without a separate tool.
precisionFlip.p3: Export preserves JPEG/WebP quality settings and uses PNG when needed. Extreme angles resample; keep 90° snaps when you only need orientation.
precisionFlip.p4: The Flip Image URL opens this same suite so SEO entry points stay distinct while the product experience stays unified.

privacy.heading: Why local-first rotation matters
privacy.p1: Orientation fixes often involve IDs, contracts, and personal photos. Keeping pixels in the page process avoids upload retention.
privacy.p2: No account wall and no server queue — progress depends on your device for deskew scoring and export.
privacy.p3: Combine with crop and compress after alignment when preparing web-ready assets.
privacy.p4: Used regularly, Rotate & Align becomes the default first step before OCR, sharing, or PDF assembly.

article.title: Create a favicon that strengthens brand identity
article.intro: A favicon is the tiny mark in browser tabs, bookmarks, and history lists. It is often the first brand cue users see after the page title — which is why a free favicon creator that starts from your real logo beats a generic letter glyph for production sites.

howToUse.heading: How to use this favicon generator online
howToUse.p1: Drop a PNG, JPG, or SVG logo. Prefer a square or near-square source with clear contrast at small sizes.
howToUse.p2: Review the live browser-tab preview and the 16×16 / 32×32 / 48×48 size grid. Toggle letterboxing if you need transparent padding instead of a center crop.
howToUse.p3: Choose multi-size .ico (legacy + broad support) or a PNG pack ZIP for modern link tags, then click Generate & Download Favicon.
howToUse.p4: Rating and review stay hidden until a successful download. Copy the HTML head snippet and place the file(s) on your server.

brand.heading: Why favicons matter for brand identity
brand.p1: Tabs fill with similar page titles; a distinct icon helps users find your site instantly among dozens of open tabs.
brand.p2: Bookmark bars and mobile home-screen shortcuts amplify brand recognition far beyond the homepage hero.
brand.p3: Keep the mark simple: complex photoreal logos blur at 16×16 — silhouette and contrast win.
brand.p4: Need a quick monogram without a logo file? Use Generate Favicon for letter-based icons, then return here when you have artwork.

htmlHead.heading: How to include the favicon in HTML <head>
htmlHead.p1: Place favicon.ico at the site root (or /public in many frameworks) so /favicon.ico resolves by convention.
htmlHead.p2: Add <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" sizes="any"> for broad compatibility.
htmlHead.p3: For PNG packs, add sized link tags such as sizes="32x32" pointing at favicon-32x32.png.
htmlHead.p4: Deploy, hard-refresh, and confirm the tab icon updates — CDNs may cache the previous icon briefly.

privacy.heading: Why local-first favicon creation matters
privacy.p1: Unreleased brand marks should not travel through a third-party icon API just to resize a square.
privacy.p2: Canvas resizing and ICO packing run in your browser memory — nothing is uploaded.
privacy.p3: No account wall for standard exports.
privacy.p4: Pair with Favicon Pack if you also need PWA / touch sizes up to 512×512.

article.intro: Raw API dumps and exported spreadsheets are hard to scan in a text editor. An online JSON viewer and CSV data explorer let you beautify structure, search nested fields, and export only the columns you need — without uploading sensitive datasets to a remote workspace.

audits.heading: Using this explorer for quick audits
audits.p1: Paste a payload, parse it, and scan the tree for unexpected nulls, oversized arrays, or misnamed keys before you write parsing code.
audits.p2: For CSV extracts from BI tools, sort a column and filter with live search to verify a slice of rows before sharing a cleaned file.
audits.p3: Copy Path to Key speeds up coding: click user.profile.settings.theme-style paths and paste them into your app or tests.
audits.p4: Keep exports lean with the column picker so stakeholders receive only relevant fields.

article.title: Clean URLs for privacy and presentation
article.intro: Shared links often carry campaign tags and click identifiers that survive far beyond the original ad or email. A privacy-friendly link stripper removes that query-string bloat so the destinations you publish look professional, reveal less about how you found the page, and paste cleanly into slides, docs, and messages. This clean URL generator also offers optional decoding so percent-encoded paths become readable without leaving your device.

privacyLinks.heading: Why clean URLs improve privacy and presentation
privacyLinks.p1: UTM tags and click IDs are useful for marketers measuring campaigns, but they are rarely useful when you bookmark a page or send a colleague a reference. Forwarding a link with utm_campaign and fbclid attached can leak referral context and make the URL look spammy in chat previews.
privacyLinks.p2: Stripping trackers keeps the protocol, host, and path intact while deleting the analytics baggage. Readers still land on the same article or product page without inheriting your campaign fingerprint.
privacyLinks.p3: Cleaner links also look better in presentations and printed materials. Short, intentional query strings (like id= or page=) stay when they are not known trackers, so functional deep links remain usable.
privacyLinks.p4: Because stripping runs locally, confidential internal URLs never leave the browser during cleanup.

utmTracking.heading: What UTM and click IDs actually do
utmTracking.p1: UTM parameters tell analytics platforms which source, medium, and campaign drove a visit. They do not change page content. Other platforms append their own tokens—Facebook’s fbclid, Google’s gclid, Microsoft’s msclkid, and many more.
utmTracking.p2: Removing those keys is safe whenever you care about the destination rather than attributed acquisition. If you still need attribution for your own campaigns, keep a tracked copy privately and share the stripped version externally.
utmTracking.p3: Decode URL turns %20 and similar encodings into readable characters for inspection. Re-sharing after decode is fine for human readability; some systems still prefer encoded spaces when the URL must be machine-parsed strictly.
utmTracking.p4: Auto-Strip on paste accelerates repetitive cleanup: enable it, paste a tracked link, and the clean URL appears immediately.

howToUse.heading: How to use this URL Parameter Stripper
howToUse.p1: Paste the full URL into the input field. Enable Decode URL if you want percent-encoding expanded into readable text.
howToUse.p2: Click Strip & Clean. Review the clean URL, note which parameters were removed, and copy the result.
howToUse.p3: Optionally enable Auto-Strip on paste for rapid cleanup of multiple links. Clear resets the session on shared machines.
howToUse.p4: After a successful strip, the rating and review block unlocks so you can leave quick product feedback.

privacy.heading: Why local-first link stripping matters
privacy.p1: URLs can contain account IDs, document tokens, or private hostnames. Sending them to a remote cleaner creates unnecessary retention risk. A local stripper keeps every character in the page process.
privacy.p2: No account wall or upload policy applies—paste, strip, and copy. Extremely exotic URI schemes still depend on what the browser’s URL parser accepts.
privacy.p3: Pair this tool with URL Encoder/Decoder when you need to rebuild query values deliberately after inspection.
privacy.p4: Used regularly, a private link stripper becomes part of a safer sharing workflow alongside JoinMyPDF’s other local utilities.

article.title: Cleaning PDF-extracted and OCR text without uploads
article.intro: Copied PDF paragraphs and OCR exports rarely look like the original document. Soft line wraps break mid-sentence, tabs and double spaces scatter alignment, zero-width characters wreck search and paste targets, and Hebrew punctuation often drifts to the wrong side after an LTR clipboard hop. A local Text Sanitizer repairs those artifacts on your device so you can move clean prose into Word, Markdown, CMS fields, or translation workflows without sending confidential drafts to a remote cleanup API.

pdfExtraction.heading: Why PDF text extraction breaks paragraphs
pdfExtraction.p1: Most PDF viewers copy text as visual lines, not logical paragraphs. That means every hard wrap in the page layout becomes a newline in your clipboard—even when the sentence is unfinished. Rejoining those soft wraps restores readability and improves spell-check and search. Sentence-ending punctuation (periods, question marks, exclamation points, and Hebrew sofim marks) is the reliable signal for where a real break should stay.
pdfExtraction.p2: Tables, captions, and multi-column layouts complicate extraction further. Prefer cleaning one paragraph region at a time when the export mixes body copy with headers and footers. After sanitation, skim the diff preview to catch joins that should have stayed separate—like address lines or code samples.
pdfExtraction.p3: Office exports and email forwards produce related noise: trailing spaces, tab-indented lines, and blank-line storms. Normalizing whitespace keeps semantics while making the paste look intentional in any editor.
pdfExtraction.p4: Because JoinMyPDF runs the sanitizer locally, you can clean contracts, medical notes, and internal policies without creating a third-party retention trail.

invisibleChars.heading: Invisible characters and rendering bugs
invisibleChars.p1: Zero-width spaces, byte-order marks, soft hyphens, and other non-printable Unicode often sneak in from PDFs, Word, and web rich-text. They are easy to miss visually but break CSS wrapping, equality checks, string search, and regex validation. Stripping them early saves hours of “why doesn’t this match?” debugging.
invisibleChars.p2: Non-breaking spaces from justified layouts can also look like ordinary spaces while refusing to wrap. Converting them to regular spaces during cleanup keeps layout tools predictable.
invisibleChars.p3: Control characters outside tabs and newlines rarely belong in prose. Removing them is safe for drafting and CMS paste; keep a raw backup if you are inspecting binary-adjacent data.
invisibleChars.p4: Use the changes preview after Clean & Fix to confirm only invisible or wrapping artifacts moved—not substance you intended to keep.

hebrewRtl.heading: Hebrew punctuation and RTL/LTR drift
hebrewRtl.p1: When Hebrew text passes through LTR editors or PDF extractors, sentence punctuation often lands at the start of the line instead of the logical end. Readers then see “.זה משפט” instead of “זה משפט.”. The Hebrew cleanup option relocates those markers for Hebrew-bearing lines while leaving Latin-only lines alone.
hebrewRtl.p2: Spaces before terminal punctuation are another common extraction artifact. Collapsing “מילה .” to “מילה.” restores expected orthography for publishing and TTS.
hebrewRtl.p3: Always review edge cases in poetry, Bible references, or mixed Hebrew/English sentences. Toggle the Hebrew option off when a document intentionally places punctuation for stylistic bidirectional effects.
hebrewRtl.p4: Pair this tool with Text Diff when you need a side-by-side audit of a larger rewrite after sanitation.

howToUse.heading: How to use this Text Sanitizer
howToUse.p1: Paste your text into the large input area. In Cleanup Options, enable the repairs you need—most PDF and OCR pastes benefit from all four defaults.
howToUse.p2: Click Clean & Fix. Review the cleaned output, copy it to your clipboard, and optionally scan the changes preview for joined lines and removed noise.
howToUse.p3: Clear resets the session on shared machines. No files leave your browser at any step.
howToUse.p4: After a successful clean, the rating and review block unlocks so you can leave quick product feedback without interrupting the cleanup flow.

privacy.heading: Why local-first text cleanup matters
privacy.p1: Paste cleanup often includes proprietary copy, customer data, and unfinished legal language. Remote “text cleaner” services create avoidable retention risk. A local engine keeps bytes in the page process.
privacy.p2: Local-first also means no account wall and no upload size policy for everyday drafts. Extremely large documents still depend on device memory—sanitize the excerpt you need when possible.
privacy.p3: Combine sanitation with redaction before sharing screenshots of cleaned output. Prefer sharing conclusions over raw confidential text.
privacy.p4: Used regularly, a private sanitizer becomes part of a safer PDF-to-editor pipeline alongside merge, OCR, and diff tools on JoinMyPDF.

article.title: Why clear writing boosts engagement and SEO
article.intro: Readers bounce when sentences wander past twenty words and when jargon stacks up without payoff. A readability score checker gives you a fast second pass before you hit publish: measure difficulty, surface the worst sentences, and simplify vocabulary while the draft still lives on your machine.

whyClarity.heading: Clarity as an engagement signal
whyClarity.p1: Accessible prose does not mean dumbing content down. It means matching sentence length and word choice to the audience so skimming scanners and careful readers both finish the page. When average words-per-sentence drifts high, comprehension and dwell time usually droop together.
whyClarity.p2: Complex words with many syllables slow scanning, especially on mobile. Flagging them visually helps writers decide which terms must stay (brand or legal language) and which can become everyday vocabulary.
whyClarity.p3: Use the score as a trend line across drafts, not an absolute grade. A technical white paper that lands near 45 can still be right for experts while a homepage hero at 45 is likely too dense.
whyClarity.p4: Pair this analyzer with word count and reading-time tools when you are shaping newsletter length or onboarding copy.

howToUse.heading: How to use this readability analyzer
howToUse.p1: Paste your draft into the large input. Click Analyze readability. The dashboard shows the Flesch Reading Ease score, estimated reading time at about 200 WPM, complexity counts, synonym suggestions, and a tone gauge.
howToUse.p2: Yellow highlights mark sentences over 20 words. Red highlights mark complex words (more than three syllables) or dictionary-matched hard vocabulary. Edit in place and re-analyze until the flags shrink.
howToUse.p3: Apply simplicity suggestions where the easier synonym still fits your voice. Keep specialized terms when accuracy matters more than grade level.
howToUse.p4: The rating and review block stays hidden until you generate your first analysis report, so feedback never interrupts an unfinished audit.

seoAccess.heading: Content clarity and SEO workflows
seoAccess.p1: Search engines reward pages people actually read. A text difficulty analyzer will not replace keyword research, but it catches copy that is needlessly hard for the searcher who just typed a question.
seoAccess.p2: Landing pages, help docs, and product descriptions benefit most: short sentences, concrete verbs, and fewer nominalizations. Aim for a standard or fairly-easy band unless the audience is specialized.
seoAccess.p3: Tone analysis is a drafting signal. If marketing copy spikes Academic markers, rewrite toward Professional or Casual before design reviews.
seoAccess.p4: Re-run the analyzer after major edits. Scores drift when you merge paragraphs or restore jargon under a deadline.

privacy.heading: Why analyze drafts locally
privacy.p1: Pre-publish drafts often include unreleased product details, pricing experiments, and customer language. Uploading them to a remote checker creates retention risk you do not need.
privacy.p2: JoinMyPDF runs syllable estimates, Flesch scoring, and synonym lookup in the browser. Nothing is sent to a server for scoring.
privacy.p3: Large pastes still depend on device memory. Analyze the section you are polishing when files are huge.
privacy.p4: Combine local readability checks with Text Sanitizer when pasted PDF or OCR text is still full of soft wraps and invisible characters.

article.title: Comparing code, configs, and drafts with a local text diff tool
article.intro: Whether you are reviewing a pull request snippet, checking an infrastructure config change, or debugging why a production file no longer matches staging, a clear text diff is one of the fastest ways to regain confidence. Version control systems already compute diffs, but you often need a lightweight browser tool for pasted fragments, exported configs, customer-provided text, or emergency troubleshooting when Git is not the right surface. This guide covers comparing code and configuration files, version control best practices that make diffs useful, and how to use a diff tool for debugging—while keeping sensitive material on your device.

comparing.heading: Comparing code and configuration files
comparing.p1: Code and configuration files look similar on disk but fail differently in production. A missing indentation in Python or YAML can change behavior more than a large comment block. A side-by-side text diff makes those differences visible immediately: green lines show what appeared in the modified version, and red lines show what disappeared from the original. When you paste two revisions of an Nginx config, Kubernetes manifest, .env sample, or TypeScript module, you can scan for accidental deletions, duplicated keys, and subtle renames without opening a full IDE.
comparing.p2: Treat configuration diffs with extra care. Infrastructure-as-code and application settings often contain hostnames, feature flags, and secrets placeholders. Even when values are not secret, the structure of a change can reveal how a system is wired. Local diffing lets you inspect those files without uploading them to a third-party comparison service. Paste the known-good version on the left and the candidate version on the right, then walk the highlighted lines before you apply the change.
comparing.p3: For source code, diffs help you answer a practical question: what actually changed relative to the last working copy? Large refactors can hide a one-line logic bug among formatting noise. Narrow the pasted range to the function or block under suspicion so the visual signal stays strong. If formatting churn overwhelms the view, normalize whitespace in your editor first, then compare again. The goal is not to replace Git—it is to make small, high-stakes comparisons readable in seconds.
comparing.p4: Configuration comparisons also catch drift between environments. When staging works and production fails, export both configs and diff them. Look for mismatched ports, feature toggles, resource limits, and dependency versions. A browser-based diff is especially helpful when the files come from different hosts, tickets, or chat threads and were never committed to the same repository. Keep Original and Modified labels honest so reviewers share the same mental model of direction.

versionControl.heading: Version control best practices
versionControl.p1: Diff tools work best when your version control habits produce clean history. Prefer small commits with focused messages so each change set tells a story. If a commit mixes formatting, dependency bumps, and business logic, reviewers will miss the important line. Before you open a pull request, skim the diff yourself—either in Git or by pasting critical sections into a local text diff—to remove accidental debug logs and leftover experiments.
versionControl.p2: Use branches as safety rails, not as dumping grounds. Keep long-lived branches rebased or merged regularly so diffs stay understandable. When conflicts appear, resolve them with the same discipline you use for reviews: understand both sides, do not blindly accept either version, and re-test after resolving. A standalone diff viewer helps when conflict markers have already been cleaned up and you need to re-check two candidate resolutions side by side.
versionControl.p3: Document why a change exists. A perfect visual diff still needs context: ticket links, rollout notes, and rollback plans. Pair that documentation with reproducible artifacts—exported configs, migration scripts, and sample inputs—so future you can regenerate the same comparison. Local-first tools fit this workflow because they never require uploading proprietary code to inspect a change.
versionControl.p4: Finally, protect secrets in version control and in ad-hoc comparisons. Never paste private keys, production credentials, or customer data into any online tool you do not trust. Prefer redacted samples when collaborating publicly. JoinMyPDF’s Text Diff runs in your browser so comparison artifacts stay on your machine, which reduces the chance that a rushed paste becomes an unintended disclosure in someone else’s logs.

debugging.heading: How to use a Diff tool for debugging
debugging.p1: Start with a hypothesis. If an API started returning errors after a deploy, gather the previous response schema and the current one, or the previous middleware config and the new one. Paste them into Original and Modified. Confirm whether the failure correlates with a deleted field, a renamed key, or an added validation rule. Diffs do not prove causality, but they quickly eliminate “nothing changed” as a theory.
debugging.p2: Use Swap when you realize you pasted the versions in the wrong order. Direction matters for reading additions and deletions. Use Clear between investigations so leftover text from an earlier incident does not contaminate the next comparison. Real-time highlighting means you can iteratively edit one pane—removing noisy comments or aligning sections—while watching the result update, which is useful when cleaning OCR’d exports or messy email forwards.
debugging.p3: For multi-file issues, diff one concern at a time. First compare the application config, then the dependency lockfile excerpt, then the feature flag payload. Mixing everything into one pane creates a wall of color that hides the signal. When the diff shows only whitespace or line-ending changes, convert both sides to the same newline style and compare again before escalating to a deeper root-cause analysis.
debugging.p4: Close the loop by writing down what the diff taught you. Add a regression test, a config assertion, or a checklist item so the same class of bug is caught earlier next time. Diff-driven debugging is most powerful when it feeds back into process: better reviews, better commits, and better environment parity checks.

howToUse.heading: How to use this Text Diff tool
howToUse.p1: Paste the baseline text into Original and the candidate text into Modified. The side-by-side result updates automatically. Green highlights mark additions in the modified version; red highlights mark deletions from the original. Stats under the controls summarize how many lines were added, removed, and left unchanged.
howToUse.p2: Click Swap to exchange the two panes if you need to reverse the comparison direction. Click Clear to reset both inputs and hide the result. Synchronized scrolling helps you keep corresponding regions aligned while reviewing long files.
howToUse.p3: Any plain text works: source code, JSON, YAML, Markdown, policies, and prose. For very large documents, compare focused sections first to keep the browser responsive. Because processing is local, you can use the tool on sensitive drafts after the page has loaded—even on restricted networks.
howToUse.p4: When you finish, clear the panes on shared workstations. Bookmark this page next to your formatter and encoder utilities so quick comparisons stay part of your everyday developer workflow.

privacy.heading: Why local-first diffing matters
privacy.p1: Text comparisons often include proprietary algorithms, unpublished product copy, incident notes, or partial secrets. Uploading that material to a remote diff service creates retention and logging risk you do not need for a line-by-line comparison. A local engine keeps the bytes in your browser process.
privacy.p2: Local-first also means fewer blockers: no account wall, no file-size upload policy, and no waiting on a server queue. The trade-off is that extremely huge files depend on your device memory—so compare the smallest useful excerpt when possible.
privacy.p3: Combine this tool with disciplined redaction. If you must share a screenshot of a diff, remove tokens and personal data first. Prefer sharing the conclusion of a comparison rather than the raw content whenever confidentiality matters.
privacy.p4: Used well, a private text diff becomes a daily instrument for safer releases, cleaner reviews, and faster debugging—without surrendering your drafts to the cloud.

article.title: HEX vs RGB vs HSL: choosing color spaces for web development
article.intro: Color is one of the most reused tokens in modern product design. A single brand blue might appear as a hex code in a style guide, an rgb() value in a CSS custom property, and an hsl() triplet in a theme engine that generates lighter and darker variants. Understanding how HEX, RGB, and HSL differ—and when each format shines—helps designers and developers keep systems consistent, accessible, and easy to maintain. Teams that treat color as a shared contract between design and engineering ship fewer “almost right” buttons and fewer dark-mode surprises. This guide explains the three color spaces, their web-development use cases, how rounding and conversion can introduce tiny drifts, and how a local online color converter fits into everyday UI work without uploading confidential brand palettes to a third-party server.

hex.heading: What HEX is and when to use it
hex.p1: HEX (hexadecimal) colors encode red, green, and blue channels as base-16 values, usually written as #RRGGBB. #2563EB means red 37, green 99, and blue 235. Design tools, marketing decks, and brand books still lean on HEX because it is compact, easy to copy, and widely recognized by non-engineers. Many component libraries also accept HEX in theme configuration files, which makes it a natural handoff format from Figma, Illustrator, or a PDF style guide into engineering tickets.
hex.p2: HEX is excellent for static tokens: brand primary, border default, surface background. It is less convenient when you need relative adjustments. Darkening a HEX value by hand requires converting to another space or guessing channel math. That is why pipelines often store a canonical value in HEX for documentation, then derive runtime variants through RGB or HSL math in code. Keeping the documented HEX and the derived scales in sync is where converters earn their keep.
hex.p3: Short HEX forms like #FFF expand to #FFFFFF. Always normalize to six digits in shared design systems to avoid ambiguity between tools that auto-expand and tools that do not. Alpha channels (#RRGGGBaa) exist in modern CSS, but many older email clients and export tools still expect opaque six-digit HEX—confirm support before shipping translucent brand tokens as HEX strings in marketing templates.
hex.p4: In collaboration, HEX remains the lingua franca. When a designer pastes #0F172A into Slack, engineers know exactly which slate to use. Keep a converter handy so you can verify that the HEX from Figma matches the RGB used in a canvas drawing API or the HSL used in a token generator. If they diverge by a unit or two after round-trips, decide which representation is source of truth and regenerate the others from it.

rgb.heading: What RGB is and when to use it
rgb.p1: RGB expresses color as three decimal channels from 0–255: rgb(37, 99, 235). Screens are additive light systems, so RGB maps naturally to how pixels are illuminated. Canvas APIs, WebGL shaders, image processing, PDF stamp overlays, and many animation libraries prefer RGB or normalized 0–1 floats derived from it. If your pipeline ever touches pixel buffers, you will almost certainly speak RGB at some layer.
rgb.p2: CSS also supports modern space-separated syntax such as rgb(37 99 235 / 0.9) with alpha. Relative color syntax in newer CSS builds on RGB-like channels and can compute hover states without leaving the stylesheet. If you interpolate colors for charts or heatmaps, RGB linear blends are common—though perceptually uniform spaces can look better for scientific visualization where equal numeric steps should look equally different.
rgb.p3: RGB is less intuitive for designers adjusting “make it a bit lighter.” Increasing all channels toward 255 lightens a color, but saturation behavior is harder to predict than in HSL. Mixing toward white or black in RGB can muddy hues. Still, RGB remains essential whenever you integrate with graphics code, email clients with limited CSS, native mobile bridges, or export pipelines that speak only channel triples.
rgb.p4: Use RGB when bridging design tokens into programmatic graphics, screenshot tooling, or PDF/canvas overlays. Convert from HEX for human-readable docs, then keep RGB for runtime drawing. A bidirectional converter prevents off-by-one mistakes when rounding between formats, especially after multiple edits in a design review cycle.

hsl.heading: What HSL is and when to use it
hsl.p1: HSL stands for hue, saturation, and lightness. Hue is the angle on the color wheel (0–360), saturation is color intensity (0–100%), and lightness moves from black to white (0–100%). hsl(217, 91%, 53%) describes a vivid blue in language that matches how people talk about color: “shift the hue,” “desaturate,” “lighten for a hover state.” That vocabulary is why HSL shows up so often in theme engines and design-system documentation.
hsl.p2: Design systems love HSL for generating ramps. Fix a hue and saturation, then step lightness to create 50–900 scales. Theme engines can flip lightness for dark mode while preserving hue relationships. Accessibility workflows often tweak lightness to meet contrast ratios without abandoning the brand hue—an operation that is awkward in HEX and noisy in RGB.
hsl.p3: HSL is not perceptually perfect—equal lightness steps do not always look equally bright across hues—but it is far more practical for UI theming than raw RGB. CSS color-mix() and relative color functions increasingly reduce the need for hand-written ramps, yet HSL remains a clear mental model for product teams explaining why a button got lighter on hover. When teaching juniors, HSL is usually the fastest way to build intuition.
hsl.p4: Choose HSL when building interactive themes, hover/focus variants, or documentation that teaches non-developers how tokens relate. Convert to HEX for handoff snapshots and to RGB for canvas work. Keeping all three in sync avoids drift between the style guide and the shipped CSS, especially when multiple people edit tokens across PRs.

useCases.heading: Practical web-development use cases
useCases.p1: Design-token pipelines: store a primary HEX for brand documentation, derive HSL for scale generation, and emit RGB for charting libraries. A converter validates that each representation still refers to the same color after rounding. Run that check before merging token PRs so production never inherits a silent one-digit drift.
useCases.p2: Accessibility audits: start from a brand HEX, move into HSL, raise lightness until text contrast passes, then copy the resulting HEX back into your theme file. Doing this locally keeps unpublished brand palettes off third-party servers and makes it easy to try several candidates quickly during a review meeting.
useCases.p3: Cross-tool handoffs: Figma may show HEX, a Lottie file expects RGB, and a Tailwind config uses HSL-like scales. Paste whichever value you have, sync the others, and copy the format the destination needs. The same workflow helps when migrating from a legacy Sass map of HEX values into a modern CSS variable system.
useCases.p4: Debugging visual bugs: when a button looks “almost right,” compare the intended token with the computed style. Converting both to HSL quickly reveals whether hue drifted, saturation collapsed, or lightness mismatched the design. Pair that with screenshot diffs so you can explain the fix in a PR with both numbers and pixels.

Invalid input shows an error on that field without wiping your previous good color until you correct it, so experimental typing stays safe.
